purpose: Records architecture, product, and governance decisions for traceability.

decisions.title: Use layered .agents governance
decisions.decision: Use docs for human intent, contracts for deterministic structure, instructions for agent implementation guidance, and execution JSON for runtime logs/state.
decisions.rationale: Separates meaning, structure, execution guidance, and runtime reporting.
consequences: Agents must consume YAML contracts for deterministic implementation.
Execution JSON must not store business logic.
Changes must follow docs → contracts → instructions → execution.

decisions.title: Keep Vouch narrow
decisions.decision: Vouch will remain a commitment-backed payment coordination system, not a marketplace, scheduler, broker, social app, escrow provider, or dispute-resolution platform.
decisions.rationale: The product is legally and operationally safer when it only coordinates deterministic payment release/refund around dual confirmation.
consequences: No public profiles, listings, reviews, ratings, messages, categories, or recommendation systems.
No dispute cases, claims, evidence uploads, appeals, or manual award actions.
Public pages must explain the mechanism without implying marketplace behavior.

decisions.title: Manual dual confirmation is MVP confirmation method
decisions.decision: MVP will use manual payer/payee confirmation during a valid confirmation window.
decisions.rationale: Manual confirmation is enough to validate the core product loop before GPS proximity or more complex presence methods.
consequences: GPS confirmation is deferred.
Both payer and payee must confirm within the window.
One-sided confirmation creates waiting state only and never releases funds.

decisions.title: Server-first architecture
decisions.decision: Use Next.js App Router with route shells in app, feature orchestration in features, pure UI in components, server reads in fetchers, server writes in actions, and Zod schemas for runtime validation.
decisions.rationale: Vouch requires server-authoritative payment, authorization, confirmation, and lifecycle state.
consequences: No raw Prisma queries in app or components.
No payment or authorization logic in UI components.
All protected fetchers and actions authenticate and authorize.

decisions.title: Use provider-backed payment state only
decisions.decision: Vouch coordinates payment state through Stripe Connect or equivalent provider infrastructure and does not directly custody funds.
decisions.rationale: Avoids direct custody and aligns product with payment coordination positioning.
consequences: Store provider references and statuses only.
Do not store raw card data, raw identity documents, or full provider payloads.
Exact Stripe flow requires payment-flow review before production.

decisions.title: Admin is operational-only
decisions.decision: Admin surfaces may inspect Vouch, user, payment, refund, webhook, notification, and audit records, but cannot arbitrate outcomes.
decisions.rationale: Admin judgment would contradict deterministic dual-confirmation rules.
consequences: No admin award funds action.
No confirmation rewrite action.
No dispute UI.
Safe retries must be idempotent and technical only.

decisions.title: Use legacy instruction filenames with Vouch-normalized meanings
decisions.decision: Retain the original instruction filenames from the requested tree while mapping legacy marketplace terms to Vouch-safe domains.
decisions.rationale: Preserves requested directory structure without violating Vouch constraints.
consequences: booking means Vouch lifecycle, not scheduling software.
guest means payer/client dashboard, not marketplace browsing.
host means payee/provider readiness or dashboard, not public provider profile.
discovery means public marketing and anti-discovery constraints.

decisions.title: Use Stripe provider-authoritative manual capture flow
decisions.decision: Vouch uses Stripe PaymentIntents with manual capture, provider-confirmed requires_capture before local authorization, Connect payout readiness from Stripe account capability state, Stripe Identity for verification, and a 5% platform fee with a $5 minimum.
decisions.rationale: Local payment/setup state must follow Stripe provider state to avoid client-reported payment readiness and unsafe release/refund behavior.
consequences: User-callable release/refund actions return system-only failures.
Expiration, reconciliation, and webhooks are responsible for deterministic provider resolution.
Production still requires Stripe sandbox and legal/payment-flow review.

decisions.title: Use Connect platform infrastructure without product marketplace behavior
decisions.decision: Vouch is a SaaS payment coordination tool that uses Stripe Connect accounts, payouts, destination charges, application fees, Identity, and fraud protection, while avoiding discovery, listings, profiles, matching, reviews, booking, escrow, brokerage, and dispute-resolution behavior.
decisions.rationale: Stripe taxonomy uses platform/marketplace to select Connect infrastructure; Vouch product taxonomy must remain payment coordination for pre-arranged appointments and in-person agreements.
consequences: Connect Accounts v2 and destination charges are allowed implementation infrastructure.
Public consumer marketplace features remain prohibited.
Stripe dashboard descriptions should use SaaS tool and payment coordination language.

decisions.title: Use sandbox Stripe accounts for implementation validation
decisions.decision: All Stripe implementation and validation work must target the sandbox platform account acct_1TPa46GV5dKxUPtb and sandbox connected account acct_1TQHpNGV5d6axbAJ until full lifecycle validation passes. The live platform account acct_1TQHH2GuFcEUvSe9 is not an implementation validation target.
decisions.rationale: The Stripe CLI and connector previously surfaced naming/account collisions between live, sandbox platform, and sandbox connected accounts. Recording the account map prevents accidental validation against the wrong platform.
consequences: Provider readiness tests must be run from a CLI profile scoped to the sandbox platform.
The sandbox connected account is confirmed ready with card_payments and transfers active.
The Stripe connector must not be used for sandbox connected-account inspection unless explicitly re-scoped to the sandbox platform.

pending_decisions.title: Adopt merchant/customer participant and pricing model
pending_decisions.question: The 2026-04-30 source-of-truth prompt supersedes prior payer/payee and platform-fee assumptions.

pending_decisions.title: Select exact Stripe Connect flow
pending_decisions.question: Superseded by DEC-008, DEC-009, and DEC-010; sandbox lifecycle review remains required before production launch.

pending_decisions.title: Define Vouch amount limits
pending_decisions.question: What minimum and maximum Vouch amounts should be enforced for MVP?

pending_decisions.title: Define confirmation window constraints
pending_decisions.question: What minimum, maximum, and default confirmation windows should be enforced?

pending_decisions.title: Select verification provider flow
pending_decisions.question: Use Stripe Identity, Connect identity requirements, Clerk metadata, or another provider for identity/adult eligibility?

pending_decisions.title: Legal copy and terms review
pending_decisions.question: What exact terms/privacy/payment language is legally approved for production?
